frameworks.name: load vs context-binding dissociation in visual working memory
frameworks.one_line: separating general attentional demand from feature-context binding at fixed load

frameworks.name: multivariate pattern analysis of delay-period fMRI
frameworks.one_line: decoding maintained feature representations from BOLD activity patterns

frameworks.name: parietal control vs occipital representation account
frameworks.one_line: IPS indexes binding demand while occipital cortex carries item content

core_question: Do parietal and occipital cortices contribute differently to control versus representational demands of nonspatial visual working memory?
key_assumptions: load can be held constant while varying cross-feature binding demand
MVPA decoding of occipital activity indexes mnemonic representational fidelity
delay-period BOLD reflects active maintenance processes
manipulations: binding context: 1 motion vs 3 motion vs 1 motion + 2 color (1M / 3M / 1M2C)
load held constant across high- and low-binding comparisons
ROI: occipital cortex vs intraparietal sulcus (with PFC extension)
key_findings: behavioral precision highest for 1M; comparable for 3M and 1M2C
occipital MVPA decoding mirrored behavior (1M > 3M ≈ 1M2C) despite equal delay-period activity
IPS delay activity tracked binding demand more than load, with caudal-to-rostral gradient
1M-to-3M rise in IPS predicted 1M-to-3M drop in behavioral and neural precision
interpretations: IPS implements context-binding control that modulates occipital representational quality
pure load sensitivity separable from binding emerges only when lateralized and extended to PFC
